About Me

Engineering the future of healthcare innovation

I am a biomedical engineer driven by a passion to be part of the inventive world of healthcare. I leverage advanced physics, computational modeling, and software tools to solve complex clinical challenges and elevate patient care. From executing high-fidelity fluid dynamics to simulate intricate neurological surgeries, to mapping the structural mechanics of medical devices, I bridge the gap between engineering theory and life-saving clinical breakthroughs.

Career Path

Where I've worked

Five roles spanning medical device quality & compliance, clinical engineering, field service, and employee advocacy. Click a card to expand the details.

MSc Radiology Coursework

Academic works — Imaging & Radiology

Quantitative laboratory investigations across four imaging modalities, completed as part of the MSc in Medical Engineering at Nottingham Trent University.

Prosthetics, Robotics & Fluid Dynamics

Computational & simulation projects

Simulation-based research spanning musculoskeletal biomechanics, bionic robotic systems, and computational fluid dynamics — built with OpenSim, ROS, ANSYS Fluent and MATLAB.

Publication

Microcontroller-based wearable interferential therapy unit for localised arthritis care

Published research on a wearable, microcontroller-driven interferential current therapy (IFC) device designed to deliver localised, non-invasive pain relief for arthritis patients.
